title: Crispy Malaysian Orange Fritters
description: A sweet, chewy, gluten‑free fritter that blends fresh orange juice and crispy batter, perfect for a sick‑day snack.
instructions: In a mixing bowl combine the gluten‑free flour, cornstarch, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon until well blended.
Whisk the fresh orange juice, honey, beaten egg, and coconut milk together in a separate bowl.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined, leaving a few small lumps.
Fold in the sliced orange rings gently so they are coated but not broken.
Heat the coconut oil in a deep‑fat pan over medium heat until it reaches 170°C.
Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil, flattening slightly, and fry until golden and crispy, about 2–3 minutes per side.
Use a slotted spoon to transfer the fritters to a paper‑towel lined plate to drain excess oil.
Optional: sprinkle chopped pistachios or a dusting of powdered sugar over the warm fritters for extra flavor.
Allow the fritters to cool for a minute before serving.
Serve hot, optionally with a drizzle of honey or a splash of additional orange juice.
